description: Defaulting targets to public visibility erodes module boundaries, letting any package depend on internals that should be private.
body: Failure mode: targets are declared with //visibility:public to silence access errors, so internal helpers become depend-able from anywhere. Detection signals: packages depend on libraries meant to be private; refactoring an internal target breaks distant consumers; the dependency graph has edges crossing intended boundaries. Do not make targets public to clear a visibility error. Safer fallback: set visibility to the specific packages or a package_group that should have access, keep helpers private, and review any change that widens visibility.
